" "The prompt_file must be located within the project root or the .control-tower directory." ) ```","user":{"login":"Copilot","name":"Copilot","email":null,"avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/946600?v=4","id":175728472},"id":2985588701,"pull_request_review_id":4003759349,"in_reply_to_id":null,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:58:05Z","review":null,"side":"RIGHT","line":null,"start_line":null,"path":"src/control_tower/prompts.py","body_html":"

_load_agent_prompt() reads prompt_file from the registry using project_root / prompt_file without constraining it to the repo directory. A malicious/accidental prompt_file like ../../.ssh/id_rsa could be read and embedded into the subagent prompt. Please validate that the resolved path stays within project_root (or within .control-tower/) before reading, and fail closed with a clear error if it does not.

Suggested change
if agent_config.get("prompt_file"):
prompt_path = project_root / str(agent_config["prompt_file"])
"""
Load the prompt for a given agent, ensuring any configured prompt_file
resides within the project root or the .control-tower directory.
"""
if agent_config.get("prompt_file"):
# Resolve the candidate prompt path and ensure it stays within an allowed root.
raw_prompt_file = str(agent_config["prompt_file"])
prompt_path = (project_root / raw_prompt_file).resolve()
project_root_resolved = project_root.resolve()
base = tower_dir(project_root).resolve()
def _is_within_directory(path: Path, root: Path) -> bool:
"""
Return True if `path` is the same as or is contained within `root`.
"""
try:
path.relative_to(root)
return True
except ValueError:
return False
if not (_is_within_directory(prompt_path, project_root_resolved) or _is_within_directory(prompt_path, base)):
raise ValueError(
f"Invalid prompt_file path for agent '{agent}': {raw_prompt_file!r}. "
"The prompt_file must be located within the project root or the .control-tower directory."
)

Pull request overview

Adds a pluggable execution layer so Tower can delegate/run agents via multiple AI runtimes (Codex/Gemini/Cursor) and expands the agent registry/init flow to support project-defined custom agents.

Changes:

  • Introduces control_tower/backends.py dispatching run_interactive() / run_exec() across codex, gemini, and cursor.
  • Extends agent registry to support per-agent backend plus custom agent entries, and updates CLI flows to accept dynamic agent names.
  • Updates prompt generation and bootstrap/init to support custom agent prompt loading and scaffolding; adds tests covering these behaviors.

Reviewed changes

backend = str(current.get('backend', ...)) can produce a default like 'None' if the registry contains backend: null. _prompt_choice returns the default unchanged when the user presses Enter, even if it isn't in the allowed options, so this can persist an invalid backend into the saved config. Consider normalizing/validating the default before prompting (e.g., fall back to 'codex' when the current value isn't in VALID_BACKENDS).

"},{"url":"https://github.com/yashturkar/control-tower/pull/17#discussion_r2985811777","body":"Same backend-default issue for existing custom agents: `str(config.get('backend', 'codex'))` can yield `'None'` (or any invalid value) and `_prompt_choice` will accept it if the user presses Enter. Same backend-default issue for existing custom agents: str(config.get('backend', 'codex')) can yield 'None' (or any invalid value) and _prompt_choice will accept it if the user presses Enter. It would be safer to coerce invalid/unknown defaults back to 'codex' (or re-prompt) before calling _prompt_choice.

Suggested change
enabled = _prompt_yes_no("Keep this agent enabled", bool(config.get("enabled", True)))
if enabled:
backend = _prompt_choice("Backend", list(VALID_BACKENDS), str(config.get("backend", "codex")))
model = _prompt_text("Model override", str(config.get("model") or ""), allow_blank=True)
dangerously_bypass = _prompt_yes_no("Use dangerous bypass", bool(config.get("dangerously_bypass", False)))
sandbox = config.get("sandbox", "workspace-write")
if not dangerously_bypass:
sandbox = _prompt_choice("Sandbox", SANDBOX_OPTIONS, str(sandbox))
else:
backend = str(config.get("backend", "codex"))
# Determine a safe default backend for this custom agent
backend_default = str(config.get("backend", "codex"))
if backend_default not in VALID_BACKENDS:
backend_default = "codex" if "codex" in VALID_BACKENDS else next(iter(VALID_BACKENDS))
enabled = _prompt_yes_no("Keep this agent enabled", bool(config.get("enabled", True)))
if enabled:
backend = _prompt_choice("Backend", list(VALID_BACKENDS), backend_default)
model = _prompt_text("Model override", str(config.get("model") or ""), allow_blank=True)
dangerously_bypass = _prompt_yes_no("Use dangerous bypass", bool(config.get("dangerously_bypass", False)))
sandbox = config.get("sandbox", "workspace-write")
if not dangerously_bypass:
sandbox = _prompt_choice("Sandbox", SANDBOX_OPTIONS, str(sandbox))
else:
backend = backend_default

Casting the backend to str means an explicitly-null/None backend in the registry becomes the literal string 'None', which then fails dispatch with a confusing "Unknown backend" error. Treat missing/falsey backend values as the default (e.g., agent_config.get('backend') or 'codex') and optionally validate earlier for a clearer error.

Suggested change
effective_backend = str(agent_config.get("backend", "codex"))
effective_backend = agent_config.get("backend") or "codex"

    path\\n        for agent_key in enabled_agents\\n        if (path := _agent_prompt_path(project_root, agent_key, registry[\\\"agents\\\"][agent_key])) is not None\\n    ]\\n    configured_agents = []\\n    for agent_key in enabled_agents:\\n        agent_config = registry[\\\"agents\\\"][agent_key]\\n        label = f\\\"- {agent_config['name']} ({agent_key})\\\"\\n        backend = agent_config.get(\\\"backend\\\", \\\"codex\\\")\\n        if backend != \\\"codex\\\":\\n            label += f\\\" [backend: {backend}]\\\"\\n        if agent_config.get(\\\"custom\\\"):\\n            label += \\\" [custom]\\\"\\n        configured_agents.append(label)\\n    if not configured_agents:\\n        configured_agents = [\\\"- No subagents enabled\\\"]\\n\\n    docs_harness = config.get(\\\"docs_harness\\\", {}) if isinstance(config, dict) else {}\\n    docs_section: list[str] = []\\n    if docs_harness.get(\\\"enabled\\\"):\\n        docs_section = [\\n            \\\"## Repo Docs Harness\\\",\\n            \\\"\\\",\\n            f\\\"- Enabled: {docs_harness.get('enabled')}\\\",\\n            f\\\"- Mode: {docs_harness.get('mode', 'unknown')}\\\",\\n            f\\\"- Doc roots: {', '.join(docs_harness.get('doc_roots', [])) or 'none'}\\\",\\n            f\\\"- Root map files: {', '.join(docs_harness.get('root_map_files', [])) or 'none'}\\\",\\n            f\\\"- Index files: {', '.join(docs_harness.get('index_files', [])) or 'none'}\\\",\\n            f\\\"- Context refs: {', '.join(docs_harness_context_refs(project_root, docs_harness))}\\\",\\n            \\\"\\\",\\n            \\\"Repo `docs/` is the durable knowledge store when this harness is enabled.\\\",\\n            \\\"`.control-tower/docs/state` and `.control-tower/memory` are operational project-state memory.\\\",\\n            \\\"After most successful Builder, Inspector, and Git-master steps, create and usually delegate a Scribe follow-up packet for repo docs unless the prior result makes that clearly unnecessary.\\\",\\n            \\\"\\\",\\n        ]\\n\\n    sections = [\\n        f\\\"You are {config.get('primary_agent', 'Tower')} for the project `{config.get('project_name', project_root.name)}`.\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        tower_prompt,\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"## Bootstrap Files\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"Read and respect these project-local agent contracts as needed:\\\",\\n        *[f\\\"- {path}\\\" for path in agent_files],\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"## Configured Agents\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        *configured_agents,\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"## Memory\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"### L0\\\",\\n        l0 or \\\"No L0 snapshot yet.\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"### L1\\\",\\n        l1 or \\\"No L1 working memory yet.\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        *docs_section,\\n        \\\"## Operating Rules\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"- Tower does not directly implement product code.\\\",\\n        \\\"- Tower delegates specialist work through typed packets.\\\",\\n        \\\"- Use `tower-run create-packet <agent> ...` to generate TaskPackets instead of writing JSON manually.\\\",\\n        \\\"- For implementation, review, research, Git, or docs work, create a packet in `.control-tower/packets/outbox/` and run `tower-run delegate <agent> --packet <path>`.\\\",\\n        \\\"- After each delegated step, read the ResultPacket, report status to the user, and seed the next handoff if needed.\\\",\\n        \\\"- After meaningful work, run `tower-run sync-memory`; use `tower-run sync-memory --emit-scribe-packet` when durable curation by Scribe is warranted.\\\",\\n        \\\"- Keep user communication concise, accurate, and traceable to repo state.\\\",\\n        \\\"- Custom agents can be delegated to exactly like built-in agents using the same packet workflow.\\\",\\n        \\\"- Agent backends (codex, gemini, cursor) are configured per-agent in the registry. The backend is selected automatically during delegation.\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"## Current Request\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        user_prompt.strip() if user_prompt else \\\"Resume control of the project and report the next best action.\\\",\\n    ]\\n    return \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(sections).strip() + \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef build_subagent_prompt(project_root: Path, agent: str, packet_text: str) -> str:\\n    base = tower_dir(project_root)\\n    registry = load_agent_registry(project_root)\\n    agent_config = registry.get(\\\"agents\\\", {}).get(agent, {})\\n\\n    prompt = _load_agent_prompt(project_root, agent, agent_config)\\n    policy = _load_agent_policy(project_root, agent, agent_config)\\n    result_schema = \\\".control-tower/schemas/packets/result.schema.json\\\"\\n\\n    sections = [\\n        prompt,\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"## Policy\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"```yaml\\\",\\n        policy,\\n        \\\"```\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"## Task Packet\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"```json\\\",\\n        packet_text.strip(),\\n        \\\"```\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        \\\"## Output Contract\\\",\\n        \\\"\\\",\\n        f\\\"Return only a JSON object that conforms to `{result_schema}`.\\\",\\n        \\\"Do not wrap the JSON in markdown.\\\",\\n        \\\"If blocked, return a valid ResultPacket with `status` set to `blocked` and explain why in `summary` and `findings`.\\\",\\n    ]\\n    return \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(sections).strip() + \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef _agent_prompt_path(project_root: Path, agent_key: str, agent_config: dict[str, object]) -> str | None:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the prompt file path for an agent, or None if no file exists.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    if agent_config.get(\\\"prompt_file\\\"):\\n        raw = str(agent_config[\\\"prompt_file\\\"])\\n        resolved = (project_root / raw).resolve()\\n        if not _is_within(resolved, project_root.resolve()):\\n            return None\\n        if resolved.exists():\\n            return raw\\n        return None\\n    default = f\\\".control-tower/agents/{agent_key}/prompt.md\\\"\\n    if (project_root / default).exists():\\n        return default\\n    return None\\n\\n\\ndef _is_within(path: Path, root: Path) -> bool:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return True if *path* is the same as or contained within *root*.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    try:\\n        path.resolve().relative_to(root.resolve())\\n        return True\\n    except ValueError:\\n        return False\\n\\n\\ndef _load_agent_prompt(project_root: Path, agent: str, agent_config: dict[str, object]) -> str:\\n    if agent_config.get(\\\"prompt_file\\\"):\\n        raw = str(agent_config[\\\"prompt_file\\\"])\\n        prompt_path = (project_root / raw).resolve()\\n        if not _is_within(prompt_path, project_root.resolve()):\\n            raise ValueError(\\n                f\\\"Invalid prompt_file path for agent '{agent}': {raw!r}. \\\"\\n                \\\"The prompt_file must be located within the project root.\\\"\\n            )\\n        content = read_text(prompt_path).strip()\\n        if content:\\n            return content\\n\\n    base = tower_dir(project_root)\\n    content = read_text(base / \\\"agents\\\" / agent / \\\"prompt.md\\\").strip()\\n    if content:\\n        return content\\n\\n    # Fallback for custom agents with no prompt file\\n    name = agent_config.get(\\\"name\\\", agent)\\n    role = agent_config.get(\\\"role\\\", \\\"custom\\\")\\n    description = agent_config.get(\\\"description\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n    return (\\n        f\\\"# {name}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"Role: {role}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"## Responsibilities\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"{description}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"## Constraints\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"- Return only a JSON ResultPacket.\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"- Do not wrap JSON in markdown.\\\\n\\\"\\n    )\\n\\n\\ndef _load_agent_policy(project_root: Path, agent: str, agent_config: dict[str, object]) -> str:\\n    base = tower_dir(project_root)\\n    content = read_text(base / \\\"agents\\\" / agent / \\\"policy.yaml\\\").strip()\\n    if content:\\n        return content\\n\\n    # Fallback for custom agents\\n    role = agent_config.get(\\\"role\\\", \\\"custom\\\")\\n    return (\\n        f\\\"agent: {agent}\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"role: {role}\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"allowed_actions:\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"  - read_files\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"  - write_files\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"  - run_commands\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"constraints:\\\\n\\\"\\n        f\\\"  - Return results as a valid ResultPacket JSON\\\\n\\\"\\n    )\\n\", \"encoding\": \"utf-8\", \"sha\": \"01f11c9bfdbce50bd30f81a7a6755b6d61153e6d\", [{"type":"text","text":"{\"content\": \"from __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport json\\nimport shutil\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\nfrom importlib import resources\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\nfrom .agents import BUILTIN_AGENT_KEYS, default_agent_registry\\nfrom .docs_harness import detect_docs_harness\\nfrom .layout import tower_dir\\nfrom .project import load_agent_registry, load_runtime_state, save_runtime_state\\n\\n\\ndef _template_root() -> Path:\\n    return Path(str(resources.files(\\\"control_tower\\\"))) / \\\"templates\\\" / \\\"project\\\"\\n\\n\\nMANAGED_TEMPLATE_PREFIXES = (\\n    Path(\\\"schemas/packets\\\"),\\n    Path(\\\"schemas/decision-graph\\\"),\\n)\\n\\n\\ndef _should_refresh_existing(relative: Path) -> bool:\\n    return any(relative.parts[: len(prefix.parts)] == prefix.parts for prefix in MANAGED_TEMPLATE_PREFIXES)\\n\\n\\ndef _merge_project_config(template_config: dict[str, object], existing_config: dict[str, object], project_root: Path) -> dict[str, object]:\\n    merged = dict(template_config)\\n    merged.update(existing_config)\\n    merged[\\\"project_name\\\"] = project_root.name\\n    merged[\\\"project_root\\\"] = str(project_root)\\n    merged[\\\"docs_harness\\\"] = detect_docs_harness(project_root, existing_config.get(\\\"docs_harness\\\", {}))\\n    return merged\\n\\n\\ndef _iso_now() -> str:\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace(\\\"+00:00\\\", \\\"Z\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef init_project(project_root: Path, force: bool = False) -> Path:\\n    destination = tower_dir(project_root)\\n    source_root = _template_root()\\n\\n    for source in source_root.rglob(\\\"*\\\"):\\n        relative = source.relative_to(source_root)\\n        target = destination / relative\\n        if source.is_dir():\\n            target.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n            continue\\n        if target.exists() and not force and not _should_refresh_existing(relative):\\n            continue\\n        target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n        shutil.copyfile(source, target)\\n\\n    project_state = destination / \\\"state\\\" / \\\"project.json\\\"\\n    template_project_state = source_root / \\\"state\\\" / \\\"project.json\\\"\\n    template_config = json.loads(template_project_state.read_text())\\n    existing_config = json.loads(project_state.read_text()) if project_state.exists() else {}\\n    config = _merge_project_config(template_config, existing_config, project_root)\\n    project_state.write_text(json.dumps(config, indent=2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    agent_registry = destination / \\\"state\\\" / \\\"agent-registry.json\\\"\\n    if not agent_registry.exists():\\n        agent_registry.write_text(json.dumps(default_agent_registry(), indent=2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n    runtime = load_runtime_state(project_root)\\n    if not runtime.get(\\\"session_import_cutoff\\\"):\\n        runtime[\\\"session_import_cutoff\\\"] = _iso_now()\\n        save_runtime_state(project_root, runtime)\\n\\n    _scaffold_custom_agent_dirs(project_root, destination)\\n\\n    return destination\\n\\n\\ndef _scaffold_custom_agent_dirs(project_root: Path, destination: Path) -> None:\\n    registry = load_agent_registry(project_root)\\n    for agent_key, agent_config in registry.get(\\\"agents\\\", {}).items():\\n        if agent_key in BUILTIN_AGENT_KEYS:\\n            continue\\n        if not agent_config.get(\\\"custom\\\"):\\n            continue\\n        agent_dir = destination / \\\"agents\\\" / agent_key\\n        agent_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n        prompt_file = agent_config.get(\\\"prompt_file\\\")\\n        if prompt_file:\\n            prompt_path = (project_root / str(prompt_file)).resolve()\\n            if not prompt_path.resolve().is_relative_to(project_root.resolve()):\\n                continue\\n            if not prompt_path.exists():\\n                prompt_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n                name = agent_config.get(\\\"name\\\", agent_key)\\n                role = agent_config.get(\\\"role\\\", \\\"custom\\\")\\n                description = agent_config.get(\\\"description\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n                prompt_path.write_text(\\n                    f\\\"# {name}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"Role: {role}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"## Responsibilities\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"{description}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"## Constraints\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"- Return only a JSON ResultPacket.\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"- Do not wrap JSON in markdown.\\\\n\\\"\\n                )\\n\", \"encoding\": \"utf-8\", \"sha\": \"e7903f553d1c859186b7b7610d7e365dfa7bb5d6\", \"display_url\": \"https://github.com/yashturkar/control-tower/blob/8dbd79906ba190e21c999b3329a2c84fa26d1fde/src/control_tower/bootstrap.py\", \"display_title\": \"bootstrap.py\"}"}]

   return f\\\"\\\"\\\"<!doctype html>\\n<html lang=\\\"en\\\">\\n<head>\\n  <meta charset=\\\"utf-8\\\" />\\n  <title>Control Tower Decision Graph</title>\\n  <style>\\n    body {{ margin: 0;    #search {{ min-width: 260px; padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid #475569; background: #0b1222; color: #e2e8f0; }}\\n    #graph {{ width: 100vw; height: calc(100vh - 58px); const counts = document.getElementById(\\\"counts\\\");\\n    const NS = \\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\";\\n    const nodes = data.nodes || [];\\n    const edges = data.edges || [];\\n    counts.textContent = `nodes: ${{nodes.length}} - edges: ${{edges.length}}`;\\n    const nodeMap = new Map(nodes.map((n) => [n.id, n]));\\n    const width = window.innerWidth;\\n    const height = window.innerHeight - 58;\\n    svg.setAttribute(\\\"viewBox\\\", `0 0 ${{width}} ${{height}}`);\\n    const g = document.createElementNS(NS, \\\"g\\\");\\n    svg.appendChild(g);\\n    const edgeLayer = document.createElementNS(NS, \\\"g\\\");\\n    const nodeLayer = document.createElementNS(NS, \\\"g\\\");\\n    g.appendChild(edgeLayer);\\n    g.appendChild(nodeLayer);\\n\\n    nodes.forEach((node, i) => {{\\n      node.x = width / 2 + Math.cos((2 * Math.PI * i) / Math.max(nodes.length, 1)) * Math.min(width, height) * 0.3;\\n      node.y = height / 2 + Math.sin((2 * Math.PI * i) / Math.max(nodes.length, 1)) * Math.min(width, height) * 0.3;\\n      node.vx = 0;\\n      node.vy = 0;\\n    }});\\n\\n    function linkEndpoints(edge) {{\\n      const from = [from, to] : null;\\n    }}\\n\\n    function render() {{\\n      edgeLayer.innerHTML = \\\"\\\";\\n      nodeLayer.innerHTML = \\\"\\\";\\n      edges.forEach((edge) => {{\\n        const pts = line.setAttribute(\\\"y1\\\", a.y);\\n        line.setAttribute(\\\"x2\\\", b.x); line.setAttribute(\\\"y2\\\", b.y);\\n        line.setAttribute(\\\"stroke\\\", \\\"#334155\\\");\\n circle.setAttribute(\\\"fill\\\", \\\"#60a5fa\\\");\\n        circle.setAttribute(\\\"data-node-id\\\", node.id);\\n        circle.style.cursor = \\\"pointer\\\";\\n        nodeLayer.appendChild(circle);\\n\\n        const label = document.createElementNS(NS, \\\"text\\\");\\n        label.setAttribute(\\\"x\\\", node.x + 10); label.setAttribute(\\\"y\\\", node.y + 4);\\n        label.setAttribute(\\\"fill\\\", \\\"#e2e8f0\\\");\\n        label.setAttribute(\\\"font-size\\\", \\\"11\\\");\\n        label.textContent = (node.title || node.subject || node.id);\\n        nodeLayer.appendChild(label);\\n\\n        circle.addEventListener(\\\"click\\\", () => {{\\n          counts.textContent = `selected: ${{node.id}} (${{node.type || \\\"unknown\\\"}}) - nodes: ${{nodes.length}} - edges: ${{edges.length}}`;\\n        }});\\n        dragNode(circle, node);\\n      }});\\n    }}\\n\\n    function tick() {{\\n      for (const a of nodes) {{\\n        for (const b of nodes) {{\\n          if (a === b) continue;\\n          const dx = a.x - b.x, dy = a.y - b.y;\\n          const d2 = Math.max(dx * dx + dy * dy, 1);\\n          const repulse = 2200 / d2;\\n          a.vx += (dx / Math.sqrt(d2)) * repulse;\\n          a.vy += (dy / Math.sqrt(d2)) * repulse;\\n        }}\\n      }}\\n      for (const edge of edges) {{\\n        const pts = linkEndpoints(edge);\\n        if (!pts) continue;\\n        const [a, b] = pts;\\n        const dx = b.x - a.x, dy = b.y - a.y;\\n        const dist = Math.max(Math.hypot(dx, dy), 1);\\n        const target = 120;\\n        const spring = (dist - target) * 0.0025;\\n        const ux = dx / dist, uy = dy / dist;\\n        a.vx += spring * ux; a.vy += spring * uy;\\n        b.vx -= spring * ux; b.vy -= spring * uy;\\n      }}\\n      for (const node of nodes) {{\\n        node.vx *= 0.88; node.vy *= 0.88;\\n        node.x = Math.min(width - 10, Math.max(10, node.x + node.vx));\\n        node.y = Math.min(height - 10, Math.max(10, node.y + node.vy));\\n      }}\\n      render();\\n      requestAnimationFrame(tick);\\n    }}\\n\\n    function dragNode(el, node) {{\\n      let dragging = false;\\n      el.addEventListener(\\\"pointerdown\\\", () => {{ dragging = true; }});\\n      window.addEventListener(\\\"pointerup\\\", () => {{ dragging = false; }});\\n      window.addEventListener(\\\"pointermove\\\", (e) => {{\\n        if (!dragging) return;\\n        const pt = svg.createSVGPoint();\\n        pt.x = e.clientX; pt.y = e.clientY;\\n        const ctm = g.getScreenCTM();\\n        if (!ctm) return;\\n        const local = pt.matrixTransform(ctm.inverse());\\n        node.x = local.x; node.y = local.y;\\n      }});\\n    }}\\n\\n    let scale = 1, tx = 0, ty = 0, panning = false, panStart = null;\\n    function applyTransform() {{ g.setAttribute(\\\"transform\\\", `translate(${{tx}},${{ty}}) scale(${{scale}})`); }}\\n    svg.addEventListener(\\\"wheel\\\", (e) => {{\\n      e.preventDefault();\\n      const delta = e.deltaY < 0 ? 1.1 : 0.9;\\n      scale = Math.max(0.2, Math.min(3, scale * delta));\\n      applyTransform();\\n    }}, {{ passive: false }});\\n    svg.addEventListener(\\\"pointerdown\\\", (e) => {{ if (e.target === svg) {{ panning = true; panStart = [e.clientX - tx, e.clientY - ty]; }} }});\\n    window.addEventListener(\\\"pointerup\\\", () => {{ panning = false; }});\\n    window.addEventListener(\\\"pointermove\\\", (e) => {{\\n      if (!panning || !panStart) return;\\n      tx = e.clientX - panStart[0];\\n      ty = e.clientY - panStart[1];\\n      applyTransform();\\n    }});\\n    document.getElementById(\\\"search\\\").addEventListener(\\\"input\\\", (e) => {{\\n      const q = e.target.value.toLowerCase().trim();\\n      for (const el of nodeLayer.querySelectorAll(\\\"circle\\\")) {{\\n        const id = el.getAttribute(\\\"data-node-id\\\");\\n        const node = nodeMap.get(id);\\n        const text = `${{node?.id || \\\"\\\"}} ${{node?.title || \\\"\\\"}} ${{node?.subject || \\\"\\\"}} ${{node?.type || \\\"\\\"}}`.toLowerCase();\\n        el.setAttribute(\\\"fill\\\", q && text.includes(q) ? \\\"#f59e0b\\\" : \\\"#60a5fa\\\");\\n      }}\\n    }});\\n    applyTransform();\\n    tick();\\n  </script>\\n</body>\\n</html>\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef _graph_payload_to_dot(payload: dict[str, object]) -> str:\\n    nodes = payload.get(\\\"nodes\\\", [])\\n    edges = payload.get(\\\"edges\\\", [])\\n    lines = [\\\"digraph decision_graph {\\\", \\\"  rankdir=LR;\\\"]\\n    for node in nodes:\\n        if not isinstance(node, dict):\\n            continue\\n        node_id = str(node.get(\\\"id\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\n        if not node_id:\\n            continue\\n        label = str(node.get(\\\"title\\\") or node.get(\\\"subject\\\") or node_id).replace('\\\"', '\\\\\\\\\\\"')\\n        node_type = str(node.get(\\\"type\\\") or \\\"node\\\").replace('\\\"', '\\\\\\\\\\\"')\\n        lines.append(f'  \\\"{node_id}\\\" [label=\\\"{label}\\\\\\\\n({node_type})\\\"];')\\n    for edge in edges:\\n        if not isinstance(edge, dict):\\n            continue\\n        from_id = edge.get(\\\"from\\\")\\n        to_id = edge.get(\\\"to\\\")\\n        if not from_id or not to_id:\\n            continue\\n        edge_type = str(edge.get(\\\"type\\\") or \\\"edge\\\").replace('\\\"', '\\\\\\\\\\\"')\\n        lines.append(f'  \\\"{from_id}\\\" -> \\\"{to_id}\\\" [label=\\\"{edge_type}\\\"];')\\n    lines.append(\\\"}\\\")\\n    return \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(lines) + \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef _graph_payload_to_svg(payload: dict[str, object]) -> str:\\n    import math\\n\\n    nodes = sorted([node for node in payload.get(\\\"nodes\\\", []) if isinstance(node, dict)], key=lambda node: str(node.get(\\\"id\\\", \\\"\\\")))\\n    edges = [edge for edge in payload.get(\\\"edges\\\", []) if isinstance(edge, dict)]\\n    width = 1200\\n    height = 900\\n    cx = width / 2\\n    cy = height / 2\\n    radius = min(width, height) * 0.36\\n    if not nodes:\\n        return (\\n            f'<svg xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\" width=\\\"{width}\\\" height=\\\"{height}\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 {width} {height}\\\">'\\n            '<rect width=\\\"100%\\\" height=\\\"100%\\\" fill=\\\"#0b1020\\\" />'\\n            '<text x=\\\"50%\\\" y=\\\"50%\\\" fill=\\\"#d1d5db\\\" text-anchor=\\\"middle\\\" font-family=\\\"sans-serif\\\" font-size=\\\"24\\\">'\\n            \\\"No graph nodes found\\\"\\n            \\\"</text></svg>\\\"\\n        )\\n    positions: dict[str, tuple[float, float]] = {}\\n    for index, node in enumerate(nodes):\\n        angle = (2 * math.pi * index) / len(nodes)\\n        positions[str(node.get(\\\"id\\\"))] = (cx + radius * math.cos(angle), cy + radius * math.sin(angle))\\n\\n    def _esc(value: str) -> str:\\n        return (\\n            value.replace(\\\"&\\\", \\\"&\\\")\\n            .replace(\\\"<\\\", \\\"<\\\")\\n            .replace(\\\">\\\", \\\">\\\")\\n            .replace('\\\"', \\\""\\\")\\n            .replace(\\\"'\\\", \\\"'\\\")\\n        )\\n\\n    parts = [\\n        f'<svg xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\" width=\\\"{width}\\\" height=\\\"{height}\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 {width} {height}\\\">',\\n        '<rect width=\\\"100%\\\" height=\\\"100%\\\" fill=\\\"#0b1020\\\" />',\\n    ]\\n    for edge in edges:\\n        from_id = str(edge.get(\\\"from\\\") or \\\"\\\")\\n        to_id = str(edge.get(\\\"to\\\") or \\\"\\\")\\n        if from_id not in positions or to_id not in positions:\\n            continue\\n        x1, y1 = positions[from_id]\\n        x2, y2 = positions[to_id]\\n        parts.append(f'<line x1=\\\"{x1:.2f}\\\" y1=\\\"{y1:.2f}\\\" x2=\\\"{x2:.2f}\\\" y2=\\\"{y2:.2f}\\\" stroke=\\\"#374151\\\" stroke-width=\\\"1.5\\\" />')\\n    for node in nodes:\\n        node_id = str(node.get(\\\"id\\\"))\\n        x, y = positions[node_id]\\n        label = _esc(str(node.get(\\\"title\\\") or node.get(\\\"subject\\\") or node_id))\\n        node_type = _esc(str(node.get(\\\"type\\\") or \\\"node\\\"))\\n        parts.append(f'<circle cx=\\\"{x:.2f}\\\" cy=\\\"{y:.2f}\\\" r=\\\"8\\\" fill=\\\"#60a5fa\\\" />')\\n        parts.append(\\n            f'<text x=\\\"{x + 12:.2f}\\\" y=\\\"{y + 4:.2f}\\\" fill=\\\"#e5e7eb\\\" font-family=\\\"sans-serif\\\" font-size=\\\"11\\\">{label} [{node_type}]</text>'\\n        )\\n    parts.append(\\\"</svg>\\\")\\n    return \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(parts) + \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef maybe_emit_scribe_docs_followup(\\n    project_root: Path,\\n    agent: str,\\n    result_packet: dict[str, object],\\n    result_path: Path,\\n) -> Path | None:\\n    config = load_project_config(project_root)\\n    docs_harness = config.get(\\\"docs_harness\\\", {}) if isinstance(config, dict) else {}\\n    if not docs_harness.get(\\\"enabled\\\"):\\n        return None\\n    if docs_harness.get(\\\"auto_scribe_mode\\\") != \\\"after-most-work\\\":\\n        return None\\n    if agent not in docs_harness.get(\\\"auto_scribe_agents\\\", []):\\n        return config[\\\"docs_harness\\\"] = ensure_docs_harness(\\n                project_root,\\n                config.get(\\\"docs_harness\\\", {}),\\n                scaffold_missing=True,\\n            )\\n           sync_and_capture_latest(project_root, role=\\\"tower\\\")\\n\\n    if args.command == \\\"resume\\\":\\n        init_project(project_root, force=False)\\n     runtime = load_runtime_state(project_root)\\n        session_id = runtime.get(\\\"last_tower_session_id\\\") or find_latest_session_id_for_project(project_root)\\n        if session_id:\\n print(\\\"L0\\\")\\n    print(l0)\\n    return 0\\n\\n\\ndef cmd_version() -> int:\\n    source_root = _source_repo_root().resolve()\\n    git_commit = _git_output(source_root, [\\\"rev-parse\\\", dict) else {}\\n    backend_arg = getattr(args, \\\"backend\\\", None)\\n    return {\\n        \\\"model\\\": args.model,\\n        \\\"sandbox\\\": args.sandbox if args.sandbox is not None else defaults.get(\\\"sandbox\\\", \\\"workspace-write\\\"),\\n        \\\"approval\\\": args.approval if args.approval is not None else defaults.get(\\\"approval\\\", \\\"on-request\\\"),\\n        \\\"search\\\": args.search if args.search is not None else bool(defaults.get(\\\"search\\\", False)),\\n        \\\"dangerous\\\": args.dangerous if args.dangerous is not None else bool(defaults.get(\\\"dangerously_bypass\\\", True)),\\n        \\\"backend\\\": backend_arg if backend_arg is not None else str(defaults.get(\\\"backend\\\", \\\"codex\\\")),\\n    }\\n\\n\\ndef _source_repo_root() -> Path:\\n    return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\\n\\n\\ndef _managed_install_repo_root() -> Path:\\n    install_base = Path(\\n        os.environ.get(\\\"CONTROL_TOWER_INSTALL_ROOT\\\")\\n        or os.environ.get(\\\"XDG_DATA_HOME\\\")\\n        or (Path.home() / \\\".local\\\" / \\\"share\\\")\\n    )\\n    if install_base.name != \\\"control-tower\\\":\\n        install_base = install_base / \\\"control-tower\\\"\\n    return install_base / \\\"repo\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef _update_installed_control_tower(source_repo_root: Path) -> Path:\\n    source_repo_root = source_repo_root.resolve()\\n    managed_repo_root = _managed_install_repo_root().resolve()\\n\\n    if source_repo_root == managed_repo_root:\\n        bootstrap_script = source_repo_root / \\\"scripts\\\" / \\\"bootstrap_remote_install.sh\\\"\\n        subprocess.run([str(bootstrap_script)], cwd=source_repo_root, check=True)\\n        return managed_repo_root\\n\\n    install_script = source_repo_root / \\\"scripts\\\" / \\\"install_tower.sh\\\"\\n    if (source_repo_root / \\\".git\\\").exists():\\n        subprocess.run([\\\"git\\\", \\\"pull\\\", \\\"--ff-only\\\"], cwd=source_repo_root, check=True)\\n    subprocess.run([str(install_script)], cwd=source_repo_root, check=True)\\n    return source_repo_root\\n\\n\\ndef _git_output(repo_root: allow_blank=True)\\n    if not name:\\n        return None\\n    key = _slugify(name)\\n    if key in existing or key in BUILTIN_AGENT_KEYS:\\n        print(f\\\"Agent key '{key}' already exists. Choose a different name.\\\")\\n        return None\\n\\n    role = _prompt_text(\\\"Role (e.g. security-review, migration, infra)\\\", \\\"custom\\\")\\n    description = _prompt_text(\\\"Description (blank to cancel)\\\", \\\"\\\", allow_blank=True)\\n    if not description:\\n        return None\\n\\n    backend, model = _prompt_backend_and_model(\\\"codex\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n    dangerously_bypass = _prompt_yes_no(\\\"Use dangerous bypass\\\", False)\\n    sandbox = \\\"workspace-write\\\"\\n    if not dangerously_bypass:\\n        sandbox = _prompt_choice(\\\"Sandbox\\\", SANDBOX_OPTIONS, \\\"workspace-write\\\")\\n\\n    prompt_file = f\\\".control-tower/agents/{key}/prompt.md\\\"\\n    use_prompt_file = _prompt_yes_no(f\\\"Create prompt file at {prompt_file}\\\", True)\\n\\n    if use_prompt_file:\\n        prompt_path = project_root / prompt_file\\n        if not prompt_path.exists():\\n            prompt_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n            prompt_path.write_text(\\n                f\\\"# {name}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                f\\\"Role: {role}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                f\\\"## Responsibilities\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                f\\\"{description}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                f\\\"## Constraints\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n                f\\\"- Return only a JSON ResultPacket.\\\\n\\\"\\n                f\\\"- Do not wrap JSON in markdown.\\\\n\\\"\\n            )\\n            # Also create a default policy.yaml\\n            policy_path = prompt_path.parent / \\\"policy.yaml\\\"\\n            if not policy_path.exists():\\n                policy_path.write_text(\\n                    f\\\"agent: {key}\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"role: {role}\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"allowed_actions:\\\\n\\\"\\n                    f\\\"  - Return results as a valid ResultPacket JSON\\\\n\\\"\\n                )\\n            print(f\\\"Created {prompt_file}\\\")\\n\\n    entry = make_custom_agent_entry(\\n        name=name,\\n        role=role,\\n        description=description,\\n        enabled=True,\\n        model=model or None,\\n        dangerously_bypass=dangerously_bypass,\\n        sandbox=sandbox,\\n        backend=backend,\\n        prompt_file=prompt_file if use_prompt_file else None,\\n    )\\n    return key, entry\\n\\n\\ndef _slugify(text: str) -> str:\\n    slug = text.lower().strip()\\n    slug = re.sub(r\\\"[^a-z0-9]+\\\", \\\"-\\\", slug)\\n    slug = slug.strip(\\\"-\\\")\\n    return slug or \\\"custom-agent\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef _print_banner() -> None:\\n    lines = list(INIT_BANNER)\\n    while lines and not lines[0].strip():\\n        lines.pop(0)\\n    while lines and not lines[-1].strip():\\n        lines.pop()\\n\\n    non_empty = [line for line in lines if line.strip()]\\n    if non_empty:\\n        left_margin = min(len(line) - len(line.lstrip(\\\" \\\")) for line in non_empty)\\n    else:\\n        left_margin = 0\\n\\n    for line in lines:\\n        print(line[left_margin:].rstrip())\\n    print(\\\"\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _print_quick_setup_notice() -> None:\\n    print(\\\"+--------------------------------------------------------------+\\\")\\n    print(\\\"|                   !!! QUICK SETUP NOTICE !!!                 |\\\")\\n    print(\\\"+--------------------------------------------------------------+\\\")\\n    print(\\\"| Builder, Inspector, Git-master, and Scribe will run in       |\\\")\\n    print(\\\"| dangerous bypass mode by default. Scout stays sandboxed.     |\\\")\\n    print(\\\"| Use `custom` now or edit `.control-tower/state/agent-        |\\\")\\n    print(\\\"| registry.json` later if you want stricter agent settings.    |\\\")\\n    print(\\\"+--------------------------------------------------------------+\\\")\\n    print(\\\"\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _prompt_text(label: str, default: str, allow_blank: bool = False) -> str:\\n    while True:\\n        raw = input(f\\\"{label} [{default}]: \\\").strip()\\n        if raw:\\n            return raw\\n        if allow_blank:\\n            return \\\"\\\"\\n        if default:\\n            return default\\n        print(\\\"A value is required. Please try again.\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _prompt_yes_no(label: str, default: bool) -> bool:\\n    suffix = \\\"Y/n\\\" if default else \\\"y/N\\\"\\n    while True:\\n        raw = input(f\\\"{label} [{suffix}]: \\\").strip().lower()\\n        if not raw:\\n            return default\\n        if raw in {\\\"y\\\", \\\"yes\\\"}:\\n            return True\\n        if raw in {\\\"n\\\", \\\"no\\\"}:\\n            return False\\n        print(\\\"Enter y or n.\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _prompt_choice(label: str, options: list[str], default: str) -> str:\\n    joined = \\\"/\\\".join(options)\\n    while True:\\n        raw = input(f\\\"{label} [{default}] ({joined}): \\\").strip()\\n        if not raw:\\n            return default\\n        if raw in options:\\n            return raw\\n        print(f\\\"Choose one of: {joined}\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _prompt_backend_and_model(\\n    default_backend: str = \\\"codex\\\",\\n    default_model: str = \\\"\\\",\\n) -> tuple[str, str]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Prompt for backend, then show curated model choices for that backend.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    backend = _prompt_choice(\\\"Backend\\\", list(VALID_BACKENDS), default_backend)\\n    models = BACKEND_MODELS.get(backend, [])\\n    if not models:\\n        model = _prompt_text(\\\"Model\\\", default_model, allow_blank=True)\\n        return backend, model\\n\\n    print(f\\\"  Models for {backend}:\\\")\\n    for i, m in enumerate(models, 1):\\n        tag = \\\" (default)\\\" if i == 1 else \\\"\\\"\\n        print(f\\\"    {i}) {m}{tag}\\\")\\n    print(f\\\"    {len(models) + 1}) custom\\\")\\n\\n    while True:\\n        current_display = default_model if default_model else models[0]\\n        raw = input(f\\\"  Model [{current_display}]: \\\").strip()\\n        if not raw:\\n            return backend, default_model or \\\"\\\"\\n        if raw.isdigit():\\n            idx = int(raw)\\n            if 1 <= idx <= len(models):\\n                return backend, models[idx - 1]\\n            if idx == len(models) + 1:\\n                custom = _prompt_text(\\\"  Custom model name\\\", \\\"\\\", allow_blank=True)\\n                return backend, custom\\n            print(f\\\"  Enter 1-{len(models) + 1} or a model name.\\\")\\n            continue\\n        # Accept a typed-in model name directly\\n        return backend, raw\\n\", \"encoding\": \"utf-8\", \"sha\": \"57529ab01a087aff0f9478ca8b73865710185026\", \"display_url\": \"https://github.com/yashturkar/control-tower/blob/8dbd79906ba190e21c999b3329a2c84fa26d1fde/src/control_tower/config_ui.py\", \"display_title\": \"config_ui.py\"}"}]

   305

+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import os
+import subprocess
+from pathlib import Path
+
+VALID_BACKENDS = ("codex", "gemini", "cursor")
+
+DEFAULT_BACKEND = "codex"
+
+
+def _common_env() -> dict[str, str]:
+    env = os.environ.copy()
+    env.setdefault("OTEL_SDK_DISABLED", "true")
+    return env
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Codex
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def _codex_base_args(
+    project_root: Path,
+    model: str | None,
+    sandbox: str | None,
+    approval: str | None,
+    search: bool,
+    dangerous: bool,
+) -> list[str]:
+    args = ["codex", "-C", str(project_root)]
+    if model:
+        args.extend(["-m", model])
+    if dangerous:
+        args.append("--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox")
+    else:
+        if sandbox:
+            args.extend(["-s", sandbox])
+        if approval:
+            args.extend(["-a", approval])
+    if search:
+        args.append("--search")
+    return args
+
+
+def _codex_interactive(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str | None,
+    resume: bool = False,
+    session_id: str | None = None,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    approval: str | None = None,
+    search: bool = False,
+    dangerous: bool = False,
+) -> int:
+    args = _codex_base_args(project_root, model, sandbox, approval, search, dangerous)
+    if resume:
+        args.append("resume")
+        if session_id:
+            args.append(session_id)
+        else:
+            args.append("--last")
+    if prompt:
+        args.append(prompt)
+    result = subprocess.run(args, cwd=project_root, env=_common_env())
+    return result.returncode
+
+
+def _codex_exec(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str,
+    output_schema: Path,
+    output_path: Path,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    dangerous: bool = False,
+) -> int:
+    args = ["codex", "exec", "-C", str(project_root), "--output-schema", str(output_schema), "-o", str(output_path)]
+    if model:
+        args.extend(["-m", model])
+    if dangerous:
+        args.append("--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox")
+    elif sandbox:
+        args.extend(["-s", sandbox])
+    args.append(prompt)
+    result = subprocess.run(args, cwd=project_root, env=_common_env())
+    return result.returncode
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Gemini
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def _gemini_interactive(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str | None,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    **_kwargs: object,
+) -> int:
+    args = ["gemini"]
+    if model:
+        args.extend(["--model", model])
+    if sandbox:
+        args.extend(["--sandbox", sandbox])
+    if prompt:
+        args.append(prompt)
+    result = subprocess.run(args, cwd=project_root, env=_common_env())
+    return result.returncode
+
+
+def _gemini_exec(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str,
+    output_schema: Path,
+    output_path: Path,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    **_kwargs: object,
+) -> int:
+    args = ["gemini", "exec", "--output-schema", str(output_schema), "-o", str(output_path)]
+    if model:
+        args.extend(["--model", model])
+    if sandbox:
+        args.extend(["--sandbox", sandbox])
+    args.append(prompt)
+    result = subprocess.run(args, cwd=project_root, env=_common_env())
+    return result.returncode
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Cursor
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def _cursor_interactive(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str | None,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    dangerous: bool = False,
+    **_kwargs: object,
+) -> int:
+    args = ["agent", "--workspace", str(project_root)]
+    if model:
+        args.extend(["--model", model])
+    if dangerous:
+        args.append("--force")
+    elif sandbox:
+        args.extend(["--sandbox", sandbox])
+    if prompt:
+        args.append(prompt)
+    result = subprocess.run(args, cwd=project_root, env=_common_env())
+    return result.returncode
+
+
+def _cursor_exec(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str,
+    output_schema: Path,
+    output_path: Path,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    dangerous: bool = False,
+    **_kwargs: object,
+) -> int:
+    args = [
+        "agent", "-p",
+        "--workspace", str(project_root),
+        "--output-format", "json",
+    ]
+    if model:
+        args.extend(["--model", model])
+    if dangerous:
+        args.append("--force")
+    elif sandbox:
+        args.extend(["--sandbox", sandbox])
+    args.append(prompt)
+    result = subprocess.run(
+        args, cwd=project_root, env=_common_env(),
+        capture_output=True, text=True,
+    )
+    if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
+        Path(output_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        Path(output_path).write_text(result.stdout)
+    return result.returncode
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Public dispatch
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def run_interactive(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str | None,
+    backend: str = DEFAULT_BACKEND,
+    resume: bool = False,
+    session_id: str | None = None,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    approval: str | None = None,
+    search: bool = False,
+    dangerous: bool = False,
+) -> int:
+    if backend == "codex":
+        return _codex_interactive(
+            project_root, prompt, resume=resume, session_id=session_id,
+            model=model, sandbox=sandbox, approval=approval,
+            search=search, dangerous=dangerous,
+        )
+    if backend == "gemini":
+        return _gemini_interactive(
+            project_root, prompt, model=model, sandbox=sandbox,
+        )
+    if backend == "cursor":
+        return _cursor_interactive(
+            project_root, prompt, model=model, sandbox=sandbox, dangerous=dangerous,
+        )
+    raise ValueError(f"Unknown backend: {backend!r}. Valid backends: {', '.join(VALID_BACKENDS)}")
+
+
+def run_exec(
+    project_root: Path,
+    prompt: str,
+    output_schema: Path,
+    output_path: Path,
+    backend: str = DEFAULT_BACKEND,
+    model: str | None = None,
+    sandbox: str | None = None,
+    dangerous: bool = False,
+) -> int:
+    if backend == "codex":
+        return _codex_exec(
+            project_root, prompt, output_schema, output_path,
+            model=model, sandbox=sandbox, dangerous=dangerous,
+        )
+    if backend == "gemini":
+        return _gemini_exec(
+            project_root, prompt, output_schema, output_path,
+            model=model, sandbox=sandbox,
+        )
+    if backend == "cursor":
+        return _cursor_exec(
+            project_root, prompt, output_schema, output_path,
+            model=model, sandbox=sandbox, dangerous=dangerous,
+        )
+    raise ValueError(f"Unknown backend: {backend!r}. Valid backends: {', '.join(VALID_BACKENDS)}")

+def _scaffold_custom_agent_dirs(project_root: Path, destination: Path) -> None:
+    registry = load_agent_registry(project_root)

+                    f"{description}\n\n"
+                    f"## Constraints\n\n"
+                    f"- Return only a JSON ResultPacket.\n"
+                    f"- Do not wrap JSON in markdown.\n"
+                )
